message: U1: IC has no bypass capacitor on VCC net
why: IC supply pins need local bypassing to avoid rail noise, resets, and unstable behavior.
repair: Add a capacitor with one pin on VCC and the other on GND.

message: U1: IC has no pin connected to literal VCC net
why: The current Stage 1 ERC only recognizes the net named exactly VCC for IC supply checks.
repair: Rename the primary positive IC rail to VCC or connect the IC supply pin to the VCC net.

message: C1: component is not reachable from any power net
why: Isolated components are usually disconnected schematic fragments and will not behave as requested.
repair: Connect the component into the intended powered signal path or remove the fragment.

message: Q1: BJT base net 'BASE_DRIVE' has no current-limiting resistor
why: A BJT base driven directly from a logic output without a series resistor can draw excessive base current and damage the driving IC or the transistor.
repair: Insert a base resistor (typically 1k-10kΩ) in series between the driver and the BJT base.

message: U1: voltage_ref OUT net 'VREF_OUT' has no bypass capacitor
why: Precision voltage references are sensitive to load transients and RF noise; a bypass capacitor at the output is required for stable reference voltage.
repair: Add a ceramic bypass capacitor (100 nF) close to the voltage reference OUT pin.

message: SW1: switch output net 'SW_OUT' has no pull-up or pull-down resistor
why: A switch output without a pull resistor leaves the signal line floating when the switch is open, causing undefined logic levels and erratic circuit behaviour.
repair: Add a pull-up resistor to VCC or a pull-down resistor to GND on the switch output net (typically 10kΩ).

message: U1: ic_power_converter VOUT net 'VOUT_RAIL' has no output capacitor
why: DC-DC converters require output capacitors for ripple filtering and stability of the control loop.
repair: Add output capacitors as specified in the converter datasheet (typically bulk electrolytic + ceramic).

message: D1: diode anode (A) is on GND and cathode (K) is on VCC - diode is reversed
why: A rectifier or protection diode installed backwards conducts in the wrong direction, clamping the supply rail or bypassing reverse-polarity protection.
repair: Flip the diode: anode (A) toward the lower potential node, cathode (K) toward the higher potential node.

message: D1: tvs_diode anode (A) is on supply net 'VCC' - TVS is forward-biased, not clamping
why: A TVS diode with its anode on the positive rail is forward biased; it clamps the line to ~0.7 V above GND, not the intended reverse-breakdown clamp voltage.
repair: Reverse the TVS: cathode (K) to the positive rail or protected node, anode (A) to GND.

message: U1: optocoupler anode net 'VCC' has no current-limiting resistor
why: The optocoupler input LED has no current limiting resistor; excessive forward current will burn out the LED and break galvanic isolation.
repair: Add a current-limiting resistor in series with the optocoupler anode (A pin), sized to keep IF within datasheet limits.

message: Y1: crystal pin 1 on net 'XTAL1' has no load capacitor
why: Crystal oscillators require load capacitors on both oscillator pins to start reliably and reach the rated frequency.
repair: Add a load capacitor (typically 18-22pF) from each crystal pin to GND.

message: D1: diode_bridge AC input pin 'AC1' is connected to a DC supply net 'VCC'
why: A diode bridge rectifier expects AC on its input pins; connecting a DC rail to the AC inputs wastes power, creates unexpected current paths, and defeats the rectification purpose.
repair: Feed the diode bridge AC input pins from a transformer secondary or AC source, not from a DC power rail.

message: SP1: speaker is connected directly to an MCU output with no audio driver
why: MCU GPIO pins typically cannot source enough current to drive a speaker; direct connection risks overloading the GPIO and producing insufficient volume.
repair: Insert an audio amplifier IC, transistor, or H-bridge driver between the MCU and the speaker.

message: U1: ic_rtc VBAT net 'VCC' has no battery or supercapacitor backup
why: An RTC without a backup power source loses the time and date the moment main power is removed, defeating the purpose of using an RTC.
repair: Connect a coin-cell battery (e.g., CR2032) or supercapacitor to the VBAT pin with a blocking diode if required.

message: BT1: battery positive terminal net 'VBAT' has no protection IC or fuse
why: An unprotected lithium battery can be over-discharged, over-charged, or short-circuited, leading to fire, swelling, or explosion.
repair: Add a battery protection IC or BMS (e.g., DW01A + FS8205) on the battery output, or at minimum a polyfuse for overcurrent protection.

message: U1: comparator OUT net 'COMP_OUT' has no pull-up resistor
why: Open-drain comparator outputs float high without a pull-up; the logic level will be indeterminate and may cause false triggers downstream.
repair: Add a pull-up resistor (typically 4.7 kΩ-10 kΩ) from the comparator OUT pin to VCC.

message: M1: DC motor has no flyback diode across its terminals
why: DC motors are inductive; switching them without flyback protection generates voltage spikes that can latch up or destroy H-bridge or driver ICs.
repair: Add flyback diodes across the motor terminals, or use an H-bridge IC with integrated protection diodes.

message: U1: level_shifter VCCA and VCCB are both on net 'VCC' - no voltage difference exists
why: A bidirectional level shifter with identical voltage on both supply rails performs no translation; signal integrity is not guaranteed and the design intent is likely wrong.
repair: Connect VCCA to one logic rail (e.g., 3.3 V) and VCCB to the other (e.g., 5 V), or replace the level shifter with a direct connection if the rails are truly identical.

message: K1: relay coil has no flyback diode across pins A1/A2
why: Relay coils are inductive; when de-energized they produce a back-EMF spike that can destroy the driving transistor or damage nearby ICs.
repair: Place a flyback diode across the relay coil pins A1/A2, cathode toward the positive rail.

message: SCR1: SCR gate net 'SCR_GATE' has no current-limiting resistor
why: An SCR gate without a resistor is susceptible to noise-triggered false firing and has no current limit, which can damage the gate junction.
repair: Add a series gate resistor (typically 47Ω-1kΩ) and optionally a pull-down to cathode.

message: D1: zener_diode anode (A) is on a positive supply net 'VCC' - diode is forward-biased
why: A zener diode with its anode on VCC is forward-biased; it acts as a standard diode rather than a voltage clamp and will draw excessive current.
repair: Reverse the zener: connect cathode (K) to the positive rail and anode (A) to GND or the lower rail.

message: D1: zener_diode cathode (K) is on a supply net 'VCC' with no series resistor
why: A zener used as a voltage clamp must have a series resistor to limit current; direct connection to a supply will burn the zener and possibly the supply.
repair: Add a series resistor between the supply and the zener cathode (K), sized so zener current stays within Pmax.

message: T1: transformer secondary net 'AC_OUT1' has no rectifier diode or bridge
why: AC from the transformer secondary must be rectified before being used as DC; without rectification, connected ICs and capacitors receive AC voltage and will be damaged.
repair: Add a rectifier diode (or diode bridge for full-wave) between the transformer secondary and the DC bus capacitor.

message: U1: ic_regulator VOUT net 'VOUT_RAIL' has no output capacitor
why: Linear regulators require an output capacitor for loop stability; without it the regulator can oscillate and destroy downstream ICs.
repair: Add a capacitor from VOUT to GND (typically 10µF ceramic or electrolytic).

message: U1: ic_regulator VIN net 'VIN_RAIL' has no input capacitor
why: Input capacitors absorb supply transients and prevent regulator dropout oscillation under load steps.
repair: Add a capacitor from VIN to GND (typically 100nF ceramic).

message: K1: solid-state relay IN+ net 'SSR_IN' has no current-limiting resistor
why: SSR input circuitry contains an internal LED; driving it without a current-limiting resistor will burn out the input optocoupler and disable the relay permanently.
repair: Add a current-limiting resistor between the driving signal and IN+, sized for the SSR input current specification (typically 5-20mA).

message: Q1: IGBT gate on net 'GATE' has no driver or bias component
why: An IGBT gate without a driver or bias resistor can turn on unpredictably from noise, leading to shoot-through or load damage.
repair: Add a gate resistor and/or dedicated IGBT gate driver IC.

message: M1: stepper motor coil is connected directly to an MCU with no stepper driver IC
why: MCU GPIO pins cannot supply the current required to drive stepper coils; direct connection will permanently damage the MCU output stages.
repair: Insert a dedicated stepper driver IC between the MCU step/dir outputs and the motor coil pins.

message: M1: servo SIG pin on net 'SIG_FLOAT' has no PWM signal source
why: A servo without a PWM control signal from an MCU, timer, or driver will not move to any commanded position and may jitter or stall unpredictably.
repair: Connect the SIG pin to a PWM-capable MCU output or dedicated RC servo controller.

message: DS1: seven_segment segment pin F on net 'GND' has no current-limiting resistor
why: Seven-segment LED segments require individual current-limiting resistors; direct connection to a logic rail will overcurrent the segment and may destroy the display or driving IC.
repair: Add a current-limiting resistor (typically 100Ω-470Ω depending on VCC and desired brightness) in series with each segment pin.

message: TR1: TRIAC gate net 'TRIAC_GATE' has no current-limiting resistor
why: A TRIAC gate without a series resistor can be noise-triggered or have unlimited gate surge current.
repair: Add a series gate resistor (typically 100Ω-1kΩ).
